name: Code Security Skills
summary: An agent skill for running and reasoning about code security scans.
why: Part of my broader interest in turning security work into developer-native workflows instead of slow, external review cycles.
demonstrates: Security automation, developer tooling, and opinionated workflows that fit how engineers actually ship.

name: 404Skill
summary: Real-world backend projects designed to teach engineering judgment, not toy CRUD.
why: The goal is to simulate the ambiguity, testing, integration, and operational tradeoffs engineers meet in production systems.
demonstrates: Curriculum design grounded in backend systems work, integration-heavy code, and operational realism.

name: Collecto
summary: An open-source, self-hosted email collection service built to stay boring, explicit, and easy to operate.
why: A small product built around a simple idea: useful backend infrastructure should favor clear behavior over platform theatre.
demonstrates: Backend product thinking, explicit operational tradeoffs, and straightforward deployment surfaces.

name: tetsu
summary: A workout logging app I use as a smaller product lab.
why: It gives me a place to test small product loops and implementation choices without pretending every project needs a grand platform story.
demonstrates: End-to-end product iteration and fast feedback, but it is intentionally not the center of the site.
